With four specialised streams, apprentices can focus their studies on the areas that matter most to their career ambitions: Construction Management Architectural Technology Building Surveying Quantity Surveying The course offers a practical education that enables students to develop the knowledge and skills for application in their specialised field. The course content and delivery reflect the prominence of personal and interpersonal skills, professional practice and behaviour, environmental, sustainable, and digital practices, together with health and safety. Throughout their academic journey, students will apply the knowledge and skills gained to design and manage real-life construction projects providing the springboard to a career as a professional within the built environment. Year 1 All streams Year one is a common year for all streams. Apprentices will be introduced to the construction industry, the environment in which it exists, the technology involved, the systems and processes which operate within it and the basic skills required by construction professionals. Year 2 Construction Management In Year 2 Construction Management, apprentices build core skills in planning and managing construction projects, integrating learning across disciplines and applying techniques to real workplace scenarios through problem-based and enquiry-led approaches. Architectural Technology In Year 2, apprentices deepen their skills in building technology, retrofit and sustainability, and BIM, applying integrated learning across disciplines through problem-based and enquiry-led approaches. Quantity Surveying In Year 2, you will be introduced to the essential skills of quantity surveying and develop your construction knowledge. You'll learn how to produce various levels of cost plans, measure buildings based on drawings, and become familiar with construction law and sustainable construction practices. Building Surveying In Year 2, apprentices will be introduced to the role of procurement and the legal framework that underpins all construction projects. You'll gain an understanding of how we design and build with Building Information Modelling (BIM). The ever-evolving role of technology in creating a better built environment will be investigated and then applied to how we reuse and adapt existing buildings to meet the needs of the future. Year 3 Construction Management In Year 3, apprentices will develop advanced skills in risk, financial, and safety management while leading interdisciplinary teams. The course focuses on the strategic application of these skills for the successful completion of a project. name: Architecture Course with Master's Degree
department: The Scott Sutherland School of Architecture & Built Environment
raw_json: {"university": {"name": "Robert Gordon University", "location": "Aberdeen, Scotland"}, "course": {"name": "Architecture Course with Master's Degree", "degree": "BSc", "level": null, "department": "The Scott Sutherland School of Architecture & Built Environment", "study_mode": ["full-time"], "duration_years": null, "start_month": null}, "fees": {"uk": {"yearly": 1820, "sandwich_year": 1820, "currency": "GBP"}, "international": {"yearly": 1820, "sandwich_year": 1820, "currency": "GBP"}}, "admission": {"ucas_code": "K100", "application_deadline": {"main": null, "late": "{ \"@context\": \"http://schema.org\", \"@type\": \"Course\", \"name\": \"Architecture\", \"url\": \"https:"}, "entry_requirements": "What You Will Study Teaching & Assessment Placements & Jobs Entry Requirements Fees & Funding The course will expose you to a range of topics where you will gain hands-on experience in a specialised setting. Years 1-3 You will develop an understanding of the basic elements and techniques of architectural design, philosophy and technology, as well as areas relating to the practice of architecture as a business. Your design skills and creativity will increase and improve, and you will learn about architectural history and theory, construction processes, materials, structures and other aspects that define human comfort. By the end of Year 3 you will have become much more independent, be able to tackle complex problems or scenarios and be ready for a period of professional practice. Check out our Stage 3 Field Trip to Comrie Croft video Year 4 An architecture education in the UK is required by the professional and statutory bodies to include 5 years of full-time learning on a professionally accredited course. In addition, in order to be eligible for the examination that awards RIBA and ARB Part 3, and which is necessary to become a legally registered architect, students are required to undertake 2 years of professional experience in the workplace. As is typical, this does not carry academic credit, however, it is an essential part of the overall learning experience required to enter the profession. It is a salaried employment and can be undertaken anywhere in the world. As is common practice in UK Schools, the first of these two years takes place between Degree and Master\u2019s level study. This forms Year 4, and is sometimes referred to as the \u2018Professional Experience Year\u2019 or \u2018Year Out\u2019. Importantly, the professional experience gained in Year 4 is recorded by the student on a regular basis and monitored by a Professional Studies Advisor within the School. This ensures that you are obtaining the appropriate breadth of experience and offers you guidance and support throughout this time. Year 5 & 6 These years offer a very different learning experience to Years 1 \u2013 3. In Years 5 and 6 your learning is characterised by extended research-led projects which address scenarios of considerable scale, scope, and complexity. You will engage with complex issues relating to the design of buildings and our environment, working individually and in groups. There will be many opportunities to pursue specialist routes, define your own interests and make decisions independently and purposefully. Student stories Hear from our students and graduates about their experiences studying this course: Student Blog - Ben Durward Graduate Story - Rachel Gray Graduate Story - Obiajulu Umeji Our sustainability commitment At its heart, Architecture is art and science of building. On one hand it requires a deep understanding of the needs and desires of people and communities, and on the other it requires consideration of structure, materials, and methods, and an understanding of the pe", "english_requirement": {"ielts": 6.5}}, "metadata": {"url": "https://www.rgu.ac.uk/study/courses/bsc-master-of-architecture", "scraped_at": "2026-05-05T10:13:10.918164+00:00"}}

name: Applied Sport and Exercise Science Course with BSc (Hons) Degree
department: School of Pharmacy, Applied Sciences and Public Health
raw_json: {"university": {"name": "Robert Gordon University", "location": "Aberdeen, Scotland"}, "course": {"name": "Applied Sport and Exercise Science Course with BSc (Hons) Degree", "degree": "BSc", "level": "undergraduate", "department": "School of Pharmacy, Applied Sciences and Public Health", "study_mode": ["full-time"], "duration_years": null, "start_month": null}, "fees": {"uk": {"yearly": 1820, "sandwich_year": 1820, "currency": "GBP"}, "international": {"yearly": 1820, "sandwich_year": 1820, "currency": "GBP"}}, "admission": {"ucas_code": "C600", "application_deadline": {"main": null, "late": "{ \"@context\": \"http://schema.org\", \"@type\": \"Course\", \"name\": \"Applied Sport and Exercise Scie"}, "entry_requirements": "What You Will Study Teaching & Assessment Placements & Jobs Entry Requirements Fees & Funding Year 1 You will study the foundations of sports and exercise science including Anatomy and Kinesiology, Exercise Physiology, Sport Psychology and Biomechanics. In addition you will also be taught a range of independent study skills required for future study. Year 2 You will apply the foundations of year 1 in a variety of different areas such as exercise programming and health improvement so that you are able to assess and improve health, fitness and performance. A work placement in year 2 will provide you with the opportunity to apply the knowledge developed in years 1 and 2. Year 3 You will look at wider applications of sports science, in terms of its use in a variety of different populations from those with chronic conditions to elite athletes. Work placements and research are a key part of year 3. Year 4 You will bring your studies together, taking a more critical look at a variety of different topics. In addition you will focus on a preferred area in your research project which forms a large part of your final year. Student stories Hear from our students and graduates about their experiences studying this course: Student Blog: Bayley Hutchison Student Blog: Josh Duncan Our sustainability commitment Exercise, sport and physical activity are fundamental to human sustainability. Underpinned by extensive scientific research, the BSc (Hons) Applied Sport and Exercise Science (ASES) is designed to develop knowledge, skills and competency in exercise, sport and physical activity for health and performance reflecting the environmental demands of the contemporary workplace and positively impacting long-term health through sustainable interventions. This course is committed to United Nation's Sustainability Goals 3, 4, 10 and 17. Find out more on the United Nations website . Events We have on campus and virtual events to help you find out more about study, student experience and future career paths. See our course and module disclaimer for more information. The university is currently undertaking an update to the course information system. This may result in minor, temporary inaccuracies, to course and module information. If you have any concerns or questions about relating to your chosen programme, please contact our admissions team . raw_json: {"university": {"name": "Robert Gordon University", "location": "Aberdeen, Scotland"}, "course": {"name": "Portfolio Application On Campus Short Course", "degree": null, "level": null, "department": "Gray's School of Art", "study_mode": ["full-time"], "duration_years": null, "start_month": null}, "fees": {"uk": {"yearly": null, "sandwich_year": null, "currency": "GBP"}, "international": {"yearly": null, "sandwich_year": null, "currency": "GBP"}}, "admission": {"ucas_code": null, "application_deadline": {"main": null, "late": "{ \"@context\": \"http://schema.org\", \"@type\": \"Course\", \"name\": \"Physical Portfolio Guidance\","}, "entry_requirements": "What You Will Study Teaching & Assessment Placements & Jobs Entry Requirements Fees & Funding Our Physical Portfolio Guidance course will take place in Gray\u2019s School of Art and remotely. The course is designed to support those looking to build a portfolio with the aim to apply to study at Gray\u2019s School of Art. The course invites creatives who wish to apply to join us on campus for a total of 5 sessions. Throughout the duration of the course, you will learn how to unpack the portfolio grading process used by Gray\u2019s School of Art. Through this, you will be taught how to evidence key mindsets and behaviours considered crucial to be a creative practitioner. The key mindsets are: The Curious Dreamer The Visual Problem Solver The Self - Reflective Communicator Explore these mindsets through a variety of different activities throughout the duration of the course. These activities will help you understand what a portfolio should include and to help you gain an understanding of what Gray\u2019s School of Art are looking for in a portfolio. You will also learn about the range of undergraduate courses available to study at Gray\u2019s School of Art, investigating the differences between them, and learning how you could adapt or tailor your portfolio to apply to different Fine Art or Design disciplines. Furthermore, you will also be provided with guidance and support around the portfolio application itself. This involves creating your own personal statement and how to document your portfolio and submit your application. raw_json: {"university": {"name": "Robert Gordon University", "location": "Aberdeen, Scotland"}, "course": {"name": "Games Design and Development Course with MSc Degree", "degree": "MSc", "level": "postgraduate", "department": "School of Computing, Engineering and Technology", "study_mode": ["full-time"], "duration_years": null, "start_month": null}, "fees": {"uk": {"yearly": null, "sandwich_year": null, "currency": "GBP"}, "international": {"yearly": null, "sandwich_year": null, "currency": "GBP"}}, "admission": {"ucas_code": null, "application_deadline": {"main": null, "late": null}, "entry_requirements": "What You Will Study Teaching & Assessment Placements & Jobs Entry Requirements Fees & Funding The MSc Games Design and Development combines creative studio practice, technical development, and professional skills to equip you with the expertise required to design, prototype, and build compelling interactive experiences. Studio-led modules encourage experimentation, critical play, and iterative design, helping you to refine ideas, test concepts, and evaluate player experience \u2013 in addition to reinforcing skills in team working and collaborative environments \u2013 aligning with industry practices. Alongside this, you will develop strong foundations in game engines, gameplay systems, real time production workflows, and interactive design methodologies. You will examine how mechanics, narrative, and user experience shape meaningful play, while gaining the technical proficiency needed to implement and iterate on your designs. The course also introduces emerging immersive technologies, offering opportunities to explore extended reality, spatial interaction, and experimental forms of digital engagement. Professional development and research training build essential skills in project planning, communication, evaluation, and entrepreneurial practice, supporting pathways into both industry roles and independent game creation. Teaching is informed by the School\u2019s research in games, immersive media, and digital creativity, ensuring learning is aligned with contemporary practice and innovation. Your studies culminate in an individual MSc project, where you integrate design, technical, and research skills to create an original game or interactive experience with creative, commercial, or societal impact. You will take part in a multidisciplinary foundation that introduces core creative and technical principles that underpin contemporary games design and development practices. This shared experience looks to help you build a strong network of peers across related disciplines and equips you with the essential skills required for more advanced studio based work. You will develop fundamental capabilities in interactive systems, prototyping, real time tools, problem solving, and digital production workflows, whilst also beginning to establish your professional practice through collaboration, reflection, and industry aligned communication skills. raw_json: {"university": {"name": "Robert Gordon University", "location": "Aberdeen, Scotland"}, "course": {"name": "Advanced Computing Course with MSc Degree", "degree": "MSc", "level": "postgraduate", "department": "School of Computing, Engineering and Technology", "study_mode": ["full-time"], "duration_years": null, "start_month": null}, "fees": {"uk": {"yearly": 1000, "sandwich_year": 1000, "currency": "GBP"}, "international": {"yearly": 1000, "sandwich_year": 1000, "currency": "GBP"}}, "admission": {"ucas_code": null, "application_deadline": {"main": null, "late": "{ \"@context\": \"http://schema.org\", \"@type\": \"Course\", \"name\": \"Advanced Computing\", \"url\": \""}, "entry_requirements": "What You Will Study Teaching & Assessment Placements & Jobs Entry Requirements Fees & Funding The MSc Advanced Computing is designed for graduates with a computing background who wish to deepen and broaden their technical expertise across contemporary computing disciplines. The course integrates advanced software engineering, cyber security, artificial intelligence, and data science, equipping you to design, build, and evaluate secure, scalable, and intelligent systems. You will explore the development of large-scale software projects, considering architecture, collaboration, and deployment in modern computing environments. Intelligent systems are examined through knowledge representation and reasoning, alongside advanced data analytics and visualisation techniques for extracting insight from complex datasets. Security is addressed from both technical and human perspectives, including secure web and database systems, user-centred security design, and governance frameworks for AI and data-driven technologies. A distinctive feature of the course is its elective structure, enabling you to tailor your studies to your career goals. You may choose to deepen your expertise in areas such as advanced AI techniques, specialised security topics, or emerging computing technologies. The course culminates in an individual project, allowing you to apply your knowledge to a substantial technical challenge aligned with your interests or professional ambitions. Learning Methods On campus students will be taught through a mixture of lectures, tutorials, group discussions, and practical lab sessions. Online students will be taught through a mixture of online recorded lectures, practical exercises, live group discussions, dedicated online support sessions, and self-study. For both study options, access to our virtual learning environment, Moodle , is also provided giving you access from home to learning materials (including videos, e-books and journals). How long is a Advanced Computing course? The MSc Advanced Computing course can be successfully completed on campus or through online learning in 12 - 16 months studying full time, or 32 months part time.
